sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Rogers Corp (NYSE:ROG) Beats Q4 Estimates but Offers Cautious Near-Term Outlook
feed.summary: Rogers Corporation (NYSE:ROG) exceeded Q4 2025 financial estimates, reporting higher net sales and significantly improved earnings per diluted share due to growth in industrial, ADAS, and renewable energy sectors. Despite strong operational performance and a healthy balance sheet, the stock's market reaction was muted due to a cautious Q1 2026 guidance, with sales and earnings forecasts slightly below analyst expectations, though management projects margin improvements and a return to growth.

feed.title: Are TPH, GLDD, AIRI, VAL Obtaining Fair Deals for their Shareholders?
feed.summary: Halper Sadeh LLC, an investor rights law firm, is investigating several companies—Tri Pointe Homes, Great Lakes Dredge & Dock Corporation, Air Industries Group, and Valaris Limited—for potential violations of federal securities laws and breaches of fiduciary duties. The firm is examining the terms of their proposed sales or mergers, suggesting that insiders might benefit more than ordinary shareholders and that the deals could limit superior offers. Shareholders of these companies are encouraged to contact Halper Sadeh LLC to understand their legal rights and options.

feed.title: Nordson (NASDAQ:NDSN) Announces Quarterly Earnings Results
feed.summary: Nordson (NASDAQ:NDSN) reported Q1 EPS of $2.37, slightly beating the consensus and raising its FY 2026 guidance to $11.00–$11.60 EPS. The company achieved record Q1 sales of approximately $669 million with 7% organic growth, indicating strong operational performance and durable demand. Despite the positive earnings, analyst consensus maintains a "Hold" rating with a target price around $266, suggesting limited near-term upside from analyst revisions alone, while institutional investors show mixed activity in their positions.

feed.title: Lockheed Martin to deliver C-130J training devices to Royal Australian Air Force
feed.summary: Lockheed Martin has secured a foreign military sale contract to provide C-130J training devices and upgrades to the Royal Australian Air Force (RAAF). Deliveries are expected to begin in 2029, enhancing the RAAF's capabilities, especially with its recent acquisition of 20 new C-130J-30 aircraft. The new simulators will feature advanced graphics and high-fidelity cockpit replication, compatible with the RAAF's expanded fleet.

feed.title: Synchrony Financial’s CareCredit Push Into Dental Workflows Meets Depressed Valuation
feed.summary: Synchrony Financial (NYSE:SYF) is expanding its CareCredit partnership with Planet DDS, integrating its financing into orthodontic and dental practice management platforms. This strategic move aims to streamline patient financing for higher-ticket treatments and embed CareCredit deeper into healthcare workflows, potentially influencing receivables and customer reach. Despite this expansion, Synchrony Financial's stock is currently trading significantly below analyst targets and Simply Wall St's fair value estimate, with forecasted earnings expected to decline over the next three years.

feed.title: Public Storage shifts HQ from California to North Texas
feed.summary: Public Storage, the nation's largest self-storage landlord, is relocating its corporate headquarters from Glendale, California, to Frisco, Texas, occupying 119,000 square feet at Hall Park. This move coincides with a leadership transition, as CFO Tom Boyle will succeed retiring CEO Joe Russell, and is part of a broader strategic reset dubbed "PS4.0" which includes a new digital operating platform. The company cited North Texas' "depth of talent and innovation" as a key factor for the relocation.

feed.title: PG&E (PCG) Pops On Guidance, Grid Spend And Dividend Hopes
feed.summary: PG&E (PCG) has reaffirmed its earnings outlook, detailed significant grid investment plans, and hinted at future dividend growth despite ongoing wildfire and regulatory risks. The company's aggressive infrastructure spending and balance sheet repair could support long-term returns, making it a hybrid play between a regulated utility and a turnaround story. Investors should weigh the potential for compounding earnings against the unique California-specific climate and policy risks.

feed.title: EUDA Health Holdings Stock Price, News & Analysis
feed.summary: EUDA Health Holdings (NASDAQ: EUDA) is a Singapore-based non-invasive healthcare provider focusing on longevity and preventive health in Asia, with operations in Singapore, Malaysia, China, and expanding into India. The company is developing stem cell therapy platforms, launching consumer wellness products like EUDA Helixé, and integrating a digital health and rewards ecosystem using a QB utility token. Additionally, EUDA also manages a property management business in Singapore and utilizes various financing instruments to support its growth.

feed.title: Host Hotels: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Host Hotels & Resorts Inc. reported strong fourth-quarter results, surpassing Wall Street's expectations for profitability and revenue. The real estate investment trust posted funds from operations of $350 million, or 51 cents per share, beating analyst estimates of 47 cents per share, and revenue of $1.6 billion, exceeding forecasts of $1.54 billion. For the upcoming year, Host Hotels anticipates full-year funds from operations between $2.03 and $2.11 per share and revenue ranging from $6.03 billion to $6.12 billion.

feed.title: F5 and Scality Expand Partnership to Deliver Secure, High-Performance Data Infrastructure for AI Workloads
feed.summary: F5 and Scality have expanded their partnership to provide a secure, high-performance data infrastructure specifically designed for AI, analytics, and data-intensive workloads. Their integrated solution combines F5’s application delivery and security platform with Scality’s S3-compatible object storage to address the growing demands of AI, ensuring faster, more resilient, and more secure data access across various environments. This collaboration aims to eliminate performance bottlenecks, scale seamlessly, and safeguard sensitive data, offering a unified architecture for secure AI data delivery.

feed.title: Equinix Announces Planned Transition of Chief Sales Officer
feed.summary: Equinix (EQIX) announced that its Chief Sales Officer, Mike Campbell, will retire on March 31, 2026, and transition into a Special Advisor role until March 5, 2027. This arrangement aims to ensure continuity in customer and revenue strategy during the leadership change. Analysts currently rate EQIX stock as a Buy, with TipRanks' AI Analyst Spark also giving it an Outperform rating due to strong fundamentals, though noting high valuation and potential near-term volatility.

feed.title: EBay is buying Depop from Etsy in a bid to boost its fashion business
feed.summary: EBay is acquiring Depop from Etsy to strengthen its fashion segment. Depop appeals to a younger demographic, with 90% of its 7 million buyers under the age of 34, according to eBay's CEO. This strategic purchase aims to boost eBay's presence in the thriving resale fashion market.

feed.title: Edison International posts strong Q4 results, raises guidance
feed.summary: Edison International reported significantly higher Q4 and full-year 2025 results, driven by Southern California Edison's rate case revenues and wildfire settlement recoveries. The company also raised its 2026 and 2027 core EPS guidance, reaffirmed its commitment to 5-7% core EPS growth through 2030, and declared a quarterly common dividend. These strong results are attributed to wildfire mitigation efforts, grid hardening, and continued shareholder returns in a highly regulated market, despite some parent-level interest and redemption losses.

feed.title: BLACKBAUD INC SEC 10-K Report
feed.summary: Blackbaud Inc. has released its annual 10-K report, detailing financial performance, business operations, and strategic initiatives. The company reported total revenue of $1,128.4 million and a net income of $115.0 million, a significant improvement from the previous year. Key highlights include a focus on AI-powered solutions, product innovation with Blackbaud AI Chat and Agents for Good™, and strategic plans for stock repurchases and operational efficiency growth through its Global Capability Center in India.

feed.title: (HST) Host Hotels & Resorts Expects 2026 Revenue Range $6.03B-$6.12B, vs. FactSet Est of $6.11B
feed.summary: Host Hotels & Resorts (HST) has provided its revenue forecast for 2026, expecting it to fall within the range of $6.03 billion to $6.12 billion. This projection aligns closely with FactSet's estimate of $6.11 billion. The announcement reflects the company's financial outlook for the upcoming year.

feed.title: The Cheesecake Factory (CAKE) Misses Q4 EPS Estimates, Shares Dr
feed.summary: The Cheesecake Factory (CAKE) reported a 4% increase in Q4 revenue but missed EPS estimates by $0.39, leading to a 7.2% drop in postmarket trading. The company's comparable restaurant sales declined, impacting net income and margins, and its financial health shows mixed signals with potential liquidity constraints and high leverage. While the Piotroski F-Score indicates a healthy financial situation, insider selling and near-high valuation metrics suggest a cautious market sentiment.

feed.title: BMO raises PPL stock price target to $41 on Kentucky rate case
feed.summary: BMO Capital has increased its price target for PPL Corp (NYSE:PPL) to $41 from $40, maintaining an Outperform rating, following the Kentucky Public Service Commission's decision on the KU/LG&E rate case. The commission modified the proposed stipulation by slightly reducing the allowed return on equity and capital riders, leading to a minor reduction in the total revenue increase. Despite these adjustments, BMO Capital analysts generally view the changes as minor and de-risking for PPL's calendar, especially with the company's earnings report coming soon and a bullish analyst consensus.

feed.title: Analog Devices: Fiscal Q1 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Analog Devices Inc. (ADI) reported strong fiscal first-quarter results, with profits of $830.8 million, or $1.69 per share, and adjusted earnings of $2.46 per share, exceeding analyst expectations. The semiconductor maker also surpassed revenue forecasts, posting $3.16 billion. Looking ahead, the company provided an optimistic outlook for the fiscal second quarter, expecting per-share earnings between $2.73 and $3.03 and revenues ranging from $3.4 billion to $3.6 billion.

feed.title: Xcel Energy seeks natural gas rate hike in North Dakota
feed.summary: Xcel Energy, through its Northern States Power subsidiary, is seeking an 11.9% natural gas rate increase in North Dakota, which would amount to an additional $10.15 per month for an average residential customer. The North Dakota Public Service Commission is reviewing the application, and an interim increase of about 10.5% ($8.42 per month) is also under consideration. This news follows the PSC's recent approval of an electric rate increase for Xcel Energy.

feed.title: Etsy Divests Depop to Refocus on Core Marketplace
feed.summary: Etsy has agreed to sell its U.K. subsidiary Depop, a mobile-first C2C fashion resale marketplace, to eBay for approximately $1.2 billion in cash. This strategic divestment allows Etsy to concentrate on its core marketplace, including share repurchases and growth investments, while strengthening eBay's position in recommerce and youth fashion. Regulatory clearances are pending, with the deal expected to close in the second quarter of 2026.

feed.title: PepsiCo’s first creator-led product launch reimagines chips for Gen Z
feed.summary: PepsiCo Foods has launched its first creator-led product line, 'Flavor Swap,' in collaboration with Madison Beer, iShowSpeed, and Dude Perfect, aiming to engage Gen Z consumers. These new chip flavor mashups are initially available on TikTok Shop with a nationwide rollout planned for March. The initiative seeks to bridge content, culture, and checkout by tapping into how Gen Z discovers and shops for products on social media platforms.

feed.title: Texas Pacific Land (NYSE:TPL) Announces Quarterly Earnings Results, Beats Expectations By $0.06 EPS
feed.summary: Texas Pacific Land (NYSE:TPL) reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst expectations with an EPS of $1.79 against a $1.73 consensus and revenues of $211.6 million against $204.0 million. The stock saw a significant jump of $12.91 to $439.33 on increased trading volume, reflecting positive investor sentiment. Despite a "Hold" consensus rating from analysts and an average target price of $350.00, institutional investors maintain a substantial ownership of 59.94% in the company.

feed.title: ANSYS Stock Is Suddenly in Play – Here’s Why Wall Street Cares
feed.summary: ANSYS Inc. has become a significant focus on Wall Street due to a multi-billion-dollar acquisition bid, transforming it from a niche engineering software company into a mainstream investment. The company's high-end simulation tools are crucial for industries like aerospace, automotive, semiconductors, and consumer electronics, playing an increasingly strategic role in AI-driven engineering and US tech development. Its stock, ANSS, is now influenced by the pending acquisition and regulatory review, making it a high-conviction bet on AI, semiconductors, and aerospace with both upside potential and regulatory risk.

feed.title: Regency Centers prices $450M senior unsecured notes offering
feed.summary: Regency Centers has priced a public offering of $450 million in senior unsecured notes due 2034. The notes will carry an interest rate of 4.950% and are being offered at 99.166% of their principal amount, resulting in a yield to maturity of 5.050%. The offering is expected to close on February 26, 2026, subject to customary closing conditions.

feed.title: What 7 Analyst Ratings Have To Say About Leidos Holdings
feed.summary: Seven analysts have recently rated Leidos Holdings (NYSE: LDOS), with an average 12-month price target of $212.71, a decrease of 3.24% from the previous average. The ratings vary from bullish to indifferent, and the company shows strong market capitalization, revenue growth, and net margin, though its Return on Equity is below industry standards. Leidos Holdings, a technology and engineering company, serves defense, intelligence, civil, and health markets.

feed.title: Cresud receives approval to issue additional shares for option exercise
feed.summary: Cresud Sociedad Anónima Comercial, Inmobiliaria, Financiera y Agropecuaria has received authorization from the Argentine Securities Commission (CNV) to issue up to 43,735,429 additional ordinary shares. This approval allows for the full exercise of outstanding options granted as part of a capital increase plan initially approved by shareholders in 2019 and the Board of Directors in 2021. If all options are exercised, Cresud’s authorized share capital for public offering will increase to ARS 725,378,233.

feed.title: CF: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: CF Industries Holdings Inc. (CF) reported strong fourth-quarter and annual results, exceeding Wall Street expectations. The company posted a Q4 profit of $404 million ($2.59 per share, adjusted to $2.99) on revenue of $1.97 billion, and an annual profit of $1.88 billion ($8.85 per share) on revenue of $7.09 billion. This performance beat analyst estimates for both earnings and revenue for the quarter.

feed.title: Wells Fargo sues California-based developer over $18M defaulted loan on Highlands Ranch office building
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has sued San Diego-based Presidio Property Trust over an $18 million defaulted loan on the Shea Center II office building in Highlands Ranch. Presidio failed to pay off the loan by January 5, leading Wells Fargo to request a receiver for the property, a request which a judge approved on February 13. The bank cited the current office market conditions, with abundant excess space and pressure to lower rents, as a reason for needing to preserve the building’s value.

feed.title: Occidental Petroleum Beats Q4 Earnings Estimates By 67%
feed.summary: Occidental Petroleum (NYSE:OXY) reported strong Q4 earnings of 31 cents per share, significantly beating analyst estimates of 18 cents by 67.57%. The company also saw a 2.71% rise in stock price in extended trading and highlighted its focus on operational excellence, cost efficiency, and debt reduction. CEO Vicki Hollub emphasized the outperformance in production and operating expenses, while the company also strengthened its balance sheet by reducing debt by $5.8 billion.

feed.title: Record AUM, Insider Buying And Platform Expansion Could Be A Game Changer For KKR (KKR)
feed.summary: KKR recently reported strong Q4 2025 results with revenue of US$5.74 billion, net income of US$1.15 billion, and record assets under management exceeding US$740 billion. A director's purchase of 50,000 shares, alongside expansion into data centers, logistics real estate, and sports investing, signals growing insider confidence and strategic platform growth. These developments underscore KKR's focus on scaling fee-generating alternatives platforms, despite potential competitive and regulatory hurdles.

feed.title: BioNTech vaccine update: what US patients should really know now
feed.summary: This article provides an update on the BioNTech/Pfizer COVID vaccine for US patients in 2026, focusing on its transition from an emergency pandemic tool to a seasonal-style protection product. It covers what's new, including updated formulations targeting current variants, clearer safety profiles, and flexible booster schedules, while addressing real-world availability, cost, and expert recommendations based on age and risk groups. The piece emphasizes the vaccine's continued effectiveness in preventing severe disease, hospitalization, and death, particularly for high-risk individuals, despite waning protection against mild infection.

feed.title: Toyota (TM) Recalls 4,400 Lexus LX 600 Vehicles Over Transmission Software Issue
feed.summary: Toyota (TM) has issued a safety recall for approximately 4,400 Lexus LX 600 vehicles from the 2025-2026 models due to a transmission software glitch that could cause power loss, accident risk, and fire hazard. Lexus dealers will perform a complimentary software update, and owners will be notified by the end of April 2026. Despite this operational challenge, Toyota maintains robust profitability and market position, though concerns about financial strength and valuation remain.

feed.title: Cheesecake Factory: Fourth Quarter Earnings Overview
feed.summary: The Cheesecake Factory Inc. announced strong fourth-quarter results, surpassing analysts' earnings and revenue predictions. The company reported a net profit of $28.8 million, with adjusted earnings of $1 per share, exceeding the anticipated 98 cents. Quarterly revenue reached $961.6 million, also outperforming forecasts.

feed.title: The Cheesecake Factory (NASDAQ:CAKE) Posts Better-Than-Expected Sales In Q4 CY2025 But Stock Drops
feed.summary: The Cheesecake Factory (NASDAQ:CAKE) reported better-than-expected Q4 CY2025 revenues of $961.6 million, a 4.4% year-on-year increase, and non-GAAP profit of $1 per share, exceeding analyst estimates. Despite these positive financial results, the stock dropped by 6.2%. The company saw a decline in same-store sales and a miss on adjusted EBITDA, indicating mixed performance for the quarter.

feed.title: Omnicom Group's (NYSE:OMC) Q4 CY2025: Beats On Revenue
feed.summary: Omnicom Group (NYSE:OMC) reported Q4 CY2025 results that exceeded revenue expectations, with sales increasing by 27.9% year-on-year to $5.53 billion. Despite beating revenue estimates, the company's non-GAAP profit per share of $2.59 was 11.8% below analysts' consensus. Omnicom's operating margin declined to -17.7%, and while its long-term EPS growth has been solid, the adjusted EPS for Q4 missed estimates.

feed.title: Nordson Corporation Reports Record First Quarter Fiscal 2026 Results and Increases Full Year Guidance
feed.summary: Nordson Corporation reported record first quarter fiscal 2026 results, with sales up 9% year-over-year to $669 million and adjusted earnings per diluted share increasing by 15% to $2.37. The company saw strong organic growth across all segments, particularly in Advanced Technology Solutions driven by electronics and x-ray systems. Based on this performance and increased backlog, Nordson has raised its full-year guidance for both sales and adjusted earnings.

feed.title: Dominion outlines options as Virginians remain in disbelief over high power bills
feed.summary: Virginians are experiencing sticker shock from unexpectedly high power bills, with some bills nearly doubling. Dominion Energy attributes the increase to intense cold temperatures and highlights options like payment plans and budget billing to help customers manage costs. Many residents, like Jim Rowe, who has implemented energy-efficient measures, are struggling to afford the rising expenses and are seeking solutions.

feed.title: Centerspace CEO says ‘a little early’ to tell if strategic review will change REIT’s 2026 strategy
feed.summary: Centerspace CEO Anne Olson stated it's "a little early" to determine if an ongoing strategic review, initiated in 2025, will alter the REIT's 2026 strategy, despite solid 2025 financial performance. The review, which could lead to a sale of the company, is focused on optimizing capital allocation and similar to actions taken by other smaller apartment REITs. Centerspace saw its net operating income grow 3.5% year over year in 2025, driven by 2.4% revenue growth.

feed.title: eBay acquires fashion resale platform Depop for $1.2B from Etsy
feed.summary: eBay is acquiring Depop, a U.K.-based fashion resale marketplace popular with younger consumers, for approximately $1.2 billion in cash from Etsy. This acquisition aims to accelerate eBay's consumer-to-consumer strategy, deepen its reach with younger, fashion-forward consumers, and expand its presence in resale. Depop is expected to retain its name, brand, platform, and culture after the transaction, which is anticipated to close in Q2 2026.

feed.title: Nordson earnings matched, revenue topped estimates
feed.summary: Nordson (NASDAQ: NDSN) reported first-quarter earnings per share that matched analyst estimates and revenue that exceeded expectations. The company also provided optimistic guidance for Q2 and the full fiscal year 2026, with revenue and EPS projections generally above current analyst consensus. Nordson's stock has shown strong performance over the last 3 and 12 months, with a "good performance" financial health score from InvestingPro.

feed.title: Agnico Eagle Boosts Strategic Stake in Maple Gold Mines
feed.summary: Agnico Eagle Mines Limited has increased its strategic stake in Maple Gold Mines Ltd. by acquiring 662,780 common shares for approximately C$1.6 million, bringing its total ownership to nearly 13% on a non-diluted basis. This investment utilizes an investor rights agreement, reinforcing Agnico Eagle's strategy to secure positions in high-potential gold projects and potentially enhance its global gold portfolio. TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates Agnico Eagle (TSE:AEM) as an Outperform due to its financial strength and growth pipeline, despite valuation concerns.

feed.title: Regency Centers Prices $450 Million Senior Unsecured Notes Offering
feed.summary: Regency Centers Corporation announced that its operating partnership has priced a $450 million public offering of senior unsecured notes due 2033. The notes, with a 4.50% coupon, were issued at 99.376% of par value. Regency intends to use the net proceeds to reduce its line of credit, repay existing 3.81% notes due May 2026, and for general corporate purposes including capital expenditures and future debt repayment.

feed.title: American Water Works Co 2025 adjusted EPS rises
feed.summary: American Water Works Co (AWK) is projected to see a rise in its adjusted earnings per share (EPS) for the year 2025. This brief report, sourced from Refinitiv via Reuters, indicates positive financial growth for the company.

feed.title: PYPL Stockholder Alert: Shareholder Rights Law Firm Robbins LLP Reminds Investors of the Class Action Lawsuit Against PayPal Holdings, Inc.
feed.summary: Robbins LLP is reminding investors of a class action lawsuit filed against PayPal Holdings, Inc. (PYPL). The lawsuit alleges that PayPal misled investors regarding its projected revenue growth and downplayed risks, particularly concerning its Branded Checkout offerings. Following disappointing Q4 2025 and full-year financial results and news of a CEO change, PayPal's stock price dropped by over 20% in one day.

feed.title: MOODYS CORP /DE/ SEC 10-K Report
feed.summary: Moody's Corporation has released its annual Form 10-K report, detailing robust financial performance in the fiscal year with total revenue of $7,718 million and net income of $2,459 million. The report highlights strong growth in both its Analytics and Investors Service segments, driven by strategic initiatives, new product launches, and global expansion, while also acknowledging significant challenges related to regulatory, legal, operational, and market risks. The company remains committed to capital management through dividends and share repurchases, and is focusing on technology integration and sustainability initiatives.

feed.title: ARMOUR Residential REIT (NYSE:ARR) Posts Earnings Results, Misses Expectations By $0.03 EPS
feed.summary: ARMOUR Residential REIT (NYSE:ARR) recently announced its quarterly earnings, reporting $0.71 EPS, which missed analyst consensus estimates by $0.03. The company generated $236.50 million in revenue for the quarter and also declared a monthly dividend of $0.24 per share, payable on March 30th to shareholders of record on March 16th.

feed.title: EXEC: Garmin Ltd Shares Jump on Very Strong Fitness Business in Q4, Upbeat 2026 Forecast
feed.summary: Garmin Ltd. reported strong Q4 2025 results, with sales and earnings exceeding guidance, primarily driven by a 42% surge in fitness wearables and double-digit growth in Marine and Aviation segments. The company achieved record consolidated revenue of $2.1 billion for the quarter and $7.25 billion for the full year 2025. Garmin also provided an upbeat forecast for 2026, projecting $7.9 billion in revenue and increasing its annual dividend by 17%.

feed.title: M&T Bank announces quarterly dividend of $1.50 per share
feed.summary: M&T Bank Corporation has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $1.50 per share on its common stock, payable on March 31, 2026, to shareholders of record on March 2, 2026. The bank has a strong history of returning value to shareholders, having maintained dividend payments for 47 consecutive years and raising them for 9 consecutive years. Recent news includes strong Q4 2025 earnings, a new regional president appointment, and an increased price target from RBC Capital.

feed.title: eBay Acquires Fashion Marketplace Depop From Etsy In $1.2B Deal
feed.summary: eBay is acquiring the fashion marketplace Depop from Etsy in a deal valued at approximately $1.2 billion, expected to close in Q2 2026. This acquisition aims to enhance eBay's consumer-to-consumer (C2C) fashion initiatives and attract younger Gen Z shoppers in the UK and US markets. Etsy stated the sale will allow them to focus on the growth of their core marketplace, shedding a venture that was previously led by ex-CEO Josh Silverman.

feed.title: Equinix (EQIX) Announces Transition of Chief Sales Officer
feed.summary: Equinix (EQIX) has announced that its Chief Sales Officer, Mike Campbell, will retire on March 31, 2026, and transition into a part-time Special Advisor role until March 5, 2027. During this transition period, Campbell will assist with duties and provide strategic advice to the Chief Customer and Revenue Officer, receiving an annual base salary of $150,000 without bonus eligibility. This change in leadership structure is expected to draw investor attention regarding its impact on the company's operations and market performance.
